issues.title: Hardcoded authentication credentials in client-side code
issues.details: Authentication codes are hardcoded in JavaScript and visible to all users. Client-side auth can be bypassed via browser console. Move authentication validation to server-side endpoints.

issues.title: Predictable token generation pattern
issues.details: Tokens follow predictable format 'dev-admin-' + code.toLowerCase(). Use cryptographically secure random tokens instead.

issues.title: Client-side session management without server validation
issues.details: Admin session state exists only in browser memory and can be manipulated. Implement proper server-side session management with secure cookies.

issues.title: Code duplication in authentication logic
issues.details: Admin Authentication card HTML duplicated between user and admin views. Authentication logic duplicated between prompt-based and card-based systems.

issues.title: Function override mechanism with timing dependency
issues.details: setTimeout-based function override is fragile and timing-dependent. Consider proper DOM event handling instead of magic number timeouts.

issues.title: Environment detection via hardcoded port numbers
issues.details: Port-based environment detection is brittle. Consider using server-provided environment information instead.

summary: Implementation successfully addresses user requirements for permanent Admin Authentication card and resolves 401 authorization issues. However, critical security vulnerabilities exist due to client-side authentication logic and hardcoded credentials. The UI/UX implementation is excellent but requires fundamental security architecture changes before production deployment.
